generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance AutonomousVehicleActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Handles motor/steering/servo actuation commands for ACC functionality in the 1/10th-scale teleoperated autonomous vehicle"
]

instance CloudBasedAccProcessingActuator : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Cloud-based resource that processes ACC computations (lead-vehicle detection, speed calculation) offloaded from on-board ECUs"
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance LiDARSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Advanced LiDAR providing real-time point cloud data for object detection and proximity tracking in ACC applications"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudData
]

instance MotorActuatorSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Motor actuation sensor tracking vehicle speed and position data for ACC control"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorActuationStateData
]

instance SteeringSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Steering sensor providing real-time steering angle data for ACC implementation"
    DTDFVocab:producedData SteeringCommandData
]

instance ServoActuatorSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Servo actuation sensor monitoring vehicle servo positions for ACC adjustments"
    DTDFVocab:producedData ServoPositionData
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Comprehensive vehicle state sensor providing speed, position, and other operational metrics"
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleOperationalMetrics
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction - Data Transmitted
instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARSensor
    DTDFVocab:producedFrequency "High-frequency (real-time)"
    DTDFVocab:dataType "Point cloud data"
]

instance MotorActuationStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorActuatorSensor
    DTDFVocab:producedFrequency "Periodic updates (100Hz-1kHz)"
    DTDFVocab:dataType "Motor/steering command states"
]

instance SteeringCommandData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SteeringSensor
    DTDFVocab:producedFrequency "Periodic updates (100Hz-1kHz)"
    DTDFVocab:dataType "Steering angle commands"
]

instance ServoPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ServoActuatorSensor
    DTDFVocab:producedFrequency "Periodic updates (100Hz-1kHz)"
    DTDFVocab:dataType "Servo position tracking"
]

instance VehicleOperationalMetrics : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
    DTDFVocab:producedFrequency "Periodic updates (100Hz-1kHz)"
    DTDFVocab:dataType "Vehicle speed, position, and state data"
]

instance ProcessedObjectTracks : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARPointCloudData
    DTDFVocab:producedFrequency "High-frequency (real-time)"
    DTDFVocab:dataType "Structured object tracks (2D/3D spatial representations)"
]

instance ActuationCommands : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom AutonomousVehicleActuator
    DTDFVocab:producedFrequency "Control loop rates (100Hz-1kHz)"
    DTDFVocab:dataType "Motor/steering/servo actuation commands"
]

instance VisualizationData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ProcessedObjectTracks, VehicleOperationalMetrics
    DTDFVocab:producedFrequency "Real-time"
    DTDFVocab:dataType "Processed data for 3D cockpit visualization"
]

// C5: Data
instance RawLiDARData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw point cloud data from LiDAR sensors before preprocessing"
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudData
]

instance ClusteringResults : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Object clustering results from LiDAR point cloud data processing"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ProcessedObjectTracks
]

instance TrackedObjectData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured object tracks generated from LiDAR clustering and tracking"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ProcessedObjectTracks
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed vehicle state data for ACC applications"
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleOperationalMetrics
]

instance ActuationCommandsData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed actuation commands for vehicle control"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ActuationCommands
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CloudBasedAccProcessingActuator
]

instance RealTimeDashboardData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed data for real-time dashboard visualization"
    DTDFVocab:fromData TrackedObjectData, VehicleStateData
]

instance HistoricalPerformanceData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Historical performance metrics for ACC system analysis"
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleOperationalMetrics
]

// C10: Models
instance LiDARPreProcessingModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Model implementing LiDAR point cloud preprocessing (clustering, noise reduction) for object detection"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Network
]

instance LiDARTrackingModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Model handling object tracking and spatial representation from LiDAR data"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Network, HybridCloudEdgeSystem
]

instance ACCProcessingModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Adaptive Cruise Control processing model implementing lead-vehicle detection and speed computation"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CloudBasedAccProcessingActuator
]

instance VisualizationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "3D visualization model for displaying vehicle state and object tracks in the cockpit"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Network, CustomWebUI
]

instance SafetyCriticalLatencyModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Model ensuring end-to-end latency compliance for safety-critical ACC operations (10ms threshold)"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EthernetNetwork
]

// C11: Enablers
instance ROS2Network : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2-based middleware for intra-system component communication between preprocessing, tracking, and visualization modules"
    DTDFVocab:enables LiDARPreProcessingModel, LiDARTrackingModel, VisualizationModel
]

instance WiFiUDPProtocol : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Wi-Fi/UDP communication protocol for data transmission between physical twin entities and digital twin components"
    DTDFVocab:enables LiDARPointCloudData, MotorActuationStateData
]

instance HybridCloudEdgeSystem : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Hybrid cloud-edge architecture for offloading ACC computations from on-board ECUs to cloud resources"
    DTDFVocab:enables CloudBasedAccProcessingActuator, ACCProcessingModel
]

instance RealTimeSynchronizationSystem : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time synchronization mechanism ensuring data consistency between physical and digital twins via ISO 23247 compliant protocols"
    DTDFVocab:enables ProcessedObjectTracks, VehicleOperationalMetrics
]

instance DatabaseSystem : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Centralized database system storing historical vehicle data and performance metrics"
    DTDFVocab:enables HistoricalPerformanceData
]

instance CustomWebUI : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Custom-built web-based user interface for real-time data visualization and remote management"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeDashboardData
]

instance CloudComputeAllocation : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Cloud-based compute resources offloading ACC processing from resource-constrained on-board ECUs"
    DTDFVocab:enables CloudBasedAccProcessingActuator, ACCProcessingModel
]

instance TeleoperationFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Teleoperation capability enabling remote control of the physical twin for validation and debugging"
    DTDFVocab:enables AutonomousVehicleActuator, SafetyCriticalLatencyModel
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization of vehicle state data (speed, position, sensor readings) for operators and developers"
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeDashboardData, TrackedObjectData
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Offloads ACC system computation (lead-vehicle detection, speed calculation) from on-board ECUs to cloud resources"
    DTDFVocab:provides ACCProcessingModel, CloudBasedAccProcessingActuator
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Enables remote control and operation of ACC systems with dynamic enable/disable, speed parameter setting, and emergency braking commands"
    DTDFVocab:provides ActuationCommandsData, SafetyCriticalLatencyModel
]

instance DataReportingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Generates reports and historical analysis of ACC-relevant metrics for operational insights"
    DTDFVocab:provides HistoricalPerformanceData
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance ObjectDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time detection of objects in vehicle's proximity for ACC applications"
]

instance SpeedAdjustmentRecommendation : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Data-driven speed adjustment recommendations based on lead vehicle distance and ACC parameters"
]

instance EmergencyBrakingAlert : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic emergency braking command triggered by safety-critical conditions"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ACCEnablementAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Enable/disable ACC functionality dynamically via remote management interface"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance SpeedParameterSetting : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Set desired speed parameters for ACC operation"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance PerformanceTrendAnalysis : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Historical performance analysis of ACC system metrics for trend identification"
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceAlert : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive maintenance alerts based on vehicle state and operational metrics"
]

// C17: Environment
instance AutonomousVehicleEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains LiDARSensor, MotorActuatorSensor, SteeringSensor, ServoActuatorSensor, VehicleStateSensor
]

instance DigitalTwinSystemEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains LiDARPreProcessingModel, LiDARTrackingModel, ACCProcessingModel, VisualizationModel,
               ROS2Network, WiFiUDPProtocol, HybridCloudEdgeSystem
]

instance ACCApplicationEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains RealTimeMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService,
               DataReportingService, CloudComputeAllocation, TeleoperationFramework
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
    **Not in Document**
  "
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system enforces **hardware-imposed synchronization constraints** with a **time accuracy requirement of ≤100 ms** between physical and digital components. This is critical for safety-critical vehicular applications, where stricter synchronization (e.g., sub-millisecond precision) is mandated by industry standards but is constrained by hardware limitations. The system likely employs **clock synchronization protocols** (e.g., NTP or PTP) to achieve this temporal alignment, though the exact protocol is not specified. The temporal granularity for services is implied to be aligned with real-time processing demands of automotive functions (e.g., sensor data streams, actuation loops), though no explicit granularity thresholds are provided beyond the 100 ms synchronization baseline."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system follows an **entity-based architecture** rooted in **ISO 23247**, where three primary entities are instantiated: **Physical Twin Entity**, **Digital Twin Entity**, and **User Entity**. Each entity is deployed on **separate hardware components**, deviating from ISO 23247’s OME (Operations Management Entity) by hosting **general automotive functionality** (e.g., Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking) alongside resource-specific tasks. The **Physical Twin Entity** encapsulates compute, sensors, and actuators, while the **Digital Twin Entity** manages data pre-processing, actuation, and device communication. The **User Entity** hosts the **User Interface FE**, split into **3D Visualization** and **Control Panel**. ROS2 packages (each implementing one or more ROS2 nodes) enable the mapping of ISO 23247 components to functional execution environments (FEs). The system’s orchestration relies on **ROS2’s distributed middleware** for inter-entity coordination, ensuring modularity and scalability. No explicit orchestration patterns (e.g., microservices, service mesh) or resource management frameworks (e.g., Kubernetes) are mentioned."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The lifecycle stages of the Digital Twin (DT) as defined by **ISO 23247:2021** (Part 2: Reference Architecture) are implicitly structured around the **Observable Manufacturing Element (OME)** lifecycle, though the standard does not explicitly enumerate stages. However, the architecture implies a **modular, entity-based development lifecycle** where:
- **Development Phases**: Entities (e.g., Data Collection & Device Control, Digital Twin Entity, User Entity) are implemented in parallel, with **Functional Entities (FEs)** assigned specific tasks (e.g., data transformation, actuation, user interfaces). The standard does not prescribe a formalized phase model but suggests iterative development via **entity decomposition** and **FE customization** (e.g., using OPC UA for interoperability).
- **Representation Types**: The **Digital Twin Entity** dynamically mirrors the OME’s state, with **real-time synchronization** enabled by the Data Collection Entity’s data streams. The standard does not specify granularity (e.g., discrete vs. continuous updates), but the **Observable OME** ensures temporal alignment with physical processes.
- **Transition Management**: Lifecycle transitions occur via **entity composition/discomposition**, where FEs are added/removed to adapt the DT to OME changes (e.g., equipment upgrades). The standard does not mandate governance frameworks but implies **version control** via modular updates (e.g., OPC UA’s change tracking).
- **Lifecycle Governance**: While not detailed, the architecture implies **ISO 23247:2021 Part 4 (Information Exchange)** compliance for **data integrity** and **interoperability**, ensuring governance through standardized protocols (e.g., OPC UA for device control/data exchange). No explicit governance tools (e.g., CMMI) are mentioned, but the modularity supports **agile adaptation**.
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The ISO 23247 framework emphasizes **validation and verification (V&V)** through the **Digital Twin Entity**, though specific methodologies are not detailed. Key considerations include:

**Model Fidelity Requirements:**
- **Accuracy:** The DT’s **Digital Twin Entity** must replicate OME behavior with high fidelity, particularly for critical functions (e.g., process control). The standard does not prescribe exact thresholds but implies adherence to domain-specific standards (e.g., ISO 9001 for manufacturing quality).

**Validation and Verification Methods:**
- **Entity-Based Validation:** Each **Functional Entity (FE)** within the **Digital Twin Entity** is validated independently (e.g., via unit tests or simulation against real OMEs).
- **Continuous Calibration:** The **Data Collection and Device Control Entity** ensures sensor data aligns with physical OMEs through **real-time feedback loops**, though no specific calibration protocols are mentioned.

**Uncertainty Sources and Quantification:**
- **Data Noise:** Errors in sensor readings or communication delays are mitigated by the **Data Collection Entity’s** filtering mechanisms (implied but unspecified).
- **Model Inaccuracy:** Behavioral models may introduce uncertainties, addressed via **error correction FEs** (e.g., adaptive learning algorithms not detailed).

**Error Detection and Correction:**
- **Automated Alerts:** The **User Entity** triggers alerts if discrepancies exceed predefined thresholds (e.g., via OPC UA or custom APIs), though no specific thresholds are provided.
- **Correction Mechanisms:** The **Data Control Entity** adjusts OME parameters dynamically to compensate for model inaccuracies (e.g., via closed-loop control systems).

**Quality Assurance Processes:**
- **ISO 23247 Compliance:** The DT system must adhere to the standard’s **entity-based RA**, ensuring modularity and interoperability. No additional QA frameworks (e.g., ISO 15535) are referenced.
- **Audit Trails:** While not detailed, the standard implies traceability for data provenance and model updates via **Functional Entities (FEs)**.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
    **Technical Network Infrastructure:**
    - **Communication Protocols:**
      - **Internal:** ROS2 (ROS 2 Network) for intra-digital twin component communication.
      - **Inter-Entity:** Wi-Fi/UDP for physical twin (vehicle) ↔ digital twin data exchange.

    - **Network Topology & Architecture:**
      - **Edge-Cloud Hybrid:** Sensors (LiDAR, cameras) on the vehicle edge process data locally before transmission to the digital twin.
      - **ROS2 Nodes:** Software components (e.g., clustering, visualization) run in a distributed ROS2 network.
      - **Layered Architecture:** Data Translation FE (ISO 23247) sits as a middleware layer for serialization/unit conversion.

    - **Bandwidth & Latency Requirements:**
      - **LiDAR Point Cloud:** High bandwidth (real-time, ~100–500 Mbps for high-resolution scans).
      - **Actuation Data:** Low latency (<10ms) for control loops (e.g., steering servo adjustments).
      - **Visualization Data:** Moderate bandwidth (~10–50 Mbps) for 3D rendering.

    - **Reliability & Redundancy:**
      - **Wi-Fi/UDP:** UDP ensures low-latency but lacks built-in redundancy; potential for packet loss handled via retransmission logic (not specified).
      - **ROS2 Topics:** Topic-based communication with message validation (e.g., checksums for critical data like vehicle state).

    - **Security Protocols:**
      - **Data Translation FE:** Serialization/unit conversion ensures data integrity during transmission.
      - **Network-Level:** Wi-Fi/UDP may use TLS for encrypted payloads (not explicitly detailed in docs).
      - **Physical Twin:** No explicit mention of authentication for sensor data (e.g., vehicle-to-cloud).
  "
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
    **Deployment Infrastructure:**
    - **Hosting Platforms:**
      - **Edge Computing:** Sensors (LiDAR, cameras) process data locally on the vehicle (on-premise).
      - **Cloud/Edge Hybrid:** Digital twin components (e.g., visualization, reporting) hosted on centralized cloud servers or edge nodes (e.g., onboard PC).

    - **Computational & Storage Requirements:**
      - **Edge Devices:**
        - **CPU:** High-performance (e.g., multi-core processors for real-time clustering/tracking).
        - **Memory:** GBs for storing point clouds and processing buffers.
        - **Storage:** Temporary storage for raw LiDAR data (e.g., 100GB+ for high-frequency scans).
      - **Cloud Servers:**
        - **CPU:** Scalable (e.g., multi-threaded for parallel processing of multiple vehicles).
        - **Storage:** TBs for archiving historical data (e.g., reports, simulations).

    - **Scalability Mechanisms:**
      - **ROS2 Multi-Robot Support:** Designed for multiple physical twins (e.g., fleet vehicles) via shared ROS2 network.
      - **Horizontal Scaling:** Cloud components can scale out (e.g., more visualization nodes) to handle concurrent vehicles.
      - **Edge Offloading:** Local processing reduces cloud load for high-volume data (e.g., LiDAR).

    - **Deployment Models Supported:**
      - **On-Premise:** Edge deployment on vehicles (e.g., autonomous cars).
      - **Cloud-Based:** Centralized digital twin hosted in data centers (e.g., for fleet management).
      - **Hybrid:** Combination of edge (real-time) and cloud (analytics/reporting).

    - **Availability & Reliability Assurance:**
      - **Redundancy:** Not explicitly detailed; assumed via ROS2 failover mechanisms or Wi-Fi hotspot redundancy.
      - **Uptime:** Real-time systems (e.g., clustering/tracking) require 99.9%+ availability (implied by criticality).
      - **Disaster Recovery:** Not specified; likely manual backups for cloud-hosted data.
  "
]

instance data_ownership_and_privacy : DTDFVocab:DataOwnershipPrivacy [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin system** must adhere to **strict data governance frameworks** to ensure compliance with **data protection regulations**, particularly in the context of **Intelligent Transport Systems (ITS)** and **v2X (Vehicle-to-Everything) services**. While the provided documents do not explicitly detail ownership policies or consent management mechanisms, **ISO 15622:2018** (Adaptive Cruise Control Systems) and **ISO 26262-1:2018** (Functional Safety) emphasize **safety-critical data handling**, implying adherence to **GDPR-like privacy standards** for autonomous and connected vehicle applications.

For **data anonymization**, the **3GPP TS 28.535** (part of 5G V2X standards) indirectly suggests **differential privacy techniques** for preserving user identities in shared data streams, though no explicit mention is made. **Service requirements for v2X services** (3GPP TS 22.185) likely mandate **encryption protocols** (e.g., **AES-256**) for data integrity and confidentiality during transmission, aligning with **ISO/IEC 27001** principles.

No explicit **data sharing agreements** or **consent management systems** are documented, but **model-driven architectures** (as referenced in *Towards a model-driven architecture for interactive digital twin cockpits*) imply **semantic interoperability frameworks** (e.g., **OWL/DL ontologies**) for secure data exchange between twin components. Thus, while **data ownership** is not explicitly defined, the system must integrate **access control via role-based authorization** (e.g., **RBAC**) to align with **ISO 26262’s safety-critical requirements**.

**Not in Document** for explicit details on consent management, but implied compliance with **GDPR/ISO 27799** via **encryption and anonymization protocols**.
"
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
The **standardization** of the Digital Twin (DT) system adheres to **ISO 23247:2021**, with the following compliance details:
- **Relevant Industry Standards**:
  - **ISO 23247:2021 (Part 2)**: Reference Architecture for DTs in manufacturing, defining entity-based decomposition and functional entities (FEs).
  - **OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA)**: Adopted for **data exchange** and **device control** between the Data Collection Entity and Digital Twin Entity, ensuring interoperability.
- **Specifications Followed**:
  - **Part 3 (Digital Representation)**: The DT’s Digital Twin Entity mirrors the Observable Manufacturing Element (OME) with fidelity to its physical state.
  - **Part 4 (Information Exchange)**: Standardized protocols (e.g., OPC UA) enable seamless data flow between entities.
- **Certification Requirements**:
  - The standard does not prescribe explicit certification but implies **compliance verification** via OPC UA’s certification programs (e.g., OPC Foundation).
- **Compliance Frameworks**:
  - **ISO 23247:2021** provides a **reference architecture** for compliance, while OPC UA ensures **interoperability** across DT entities.
- **Interoperability Standards**:
  - **OPC UA** is the primary standard for DT interoperability, enabling real-time data exchange and device control.
- **Additional Context**:
  - The **public demonstration** (Document 1) references a **GitHub repository** ([McSCert/Automotive-Digital-Twin](https://github.com/McSCert/Automotive-Digital-Twin)) and a **YouTube video** ([link](https://youtu.be/Rv-sJJQc3WU)), suggesting real-world adoption of ISO 23247 and OPC UA in automotive manufacturing.
"
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin system** must incorporate **cybersecurity protocols** and **safety-critical mechanisms** aligned with **ISO 15622:2018** (Adaptive Cruise Control) and **ISO 26262-1:2018** (Functional Safety) standards. **ISO 15622** specifies **performance requirements** for adaptive cruise control, implying **real-time threat detection** (e.g., **intrusion detection systems (IDS)**) to prevent malicious interference in autonomous driving systems.

For **access control mechanisms**, **ISO 26262** mandates **hardware security modules (HSMs)** and **secure boot processes** to ensure tamper-proof execution of safety-critical code. **3GPP TS 22.185** (v2X services) introduces **authentication systems** via **X.509 certificates** and **pre-shared keys (PSKs)** for secure communication between vehicles and infrastructure, with **fail-safe mechanisms** (e.g., **fail-open/fail-safe modes**) to prevent catastrophic failures.

**Threat mitigation strategies** include **formal verification techniques** (as implied by **ISO 26262’s ASIL-based risk assessment**) and **network segmentation** to isolate vulnerable components. The **model-driven architecture** paper (*Towards a model-driven architecture for interactive digital twin cockpits*) suggests **runtime monitoring** via **event-driven architectures** (e.g., **Akka Streaming**) to detect anomalies in real-time.

**Not in Document** for explicit details on **risk assessment methodologies** beyond ISO 26262’s ASIL (Automotive Safety Integrity Level) framework, but the system must integrate **automated vulnerability scanning** (e.g., **OWASP ZAP**) for continuous security auditing.
"
]
